context: Tests whether the agent surfaces a missing shortener configuration instead of silently degrading to a raw URL. Missing config is not the same as intentional opt-out. Also tests that the shownotes URL slug comes from the Presentation Spec, not agent-invented.

checklist.name: Agent surfaces missing shortener config
checklist.description: The agent explicitly identifies that the shortener key is missing from the speaker profile's qr_code config and communicates this to the user. Must not silently proceed with a raw URL

checklist.name: Agent asks user to choose a shortener
checklist.description: The agent asks the user to choose between bitly, rebrandly, or explicitly 'none' — presenting it as a decision, not a silent default. The agent does not assume 'none' on behalf of the user

checklist.name: Agent does NOT generate QR with raw URL without asking
checklist.description: The agent does not produce a QR code encoding the raw shownotes URL without first getting explicit user confirmation that no shortener is desired. Silent raw-URL generation = zero points

checklist.name: Shownotes URL uses slug from Presentation Spec
checklist.description: The shownotes URL is constructed as https://jbaru.ch/2026-04-16-devnexus-robocoders-judgment-day, using the exact slug from presentation-spec.md. The agent does not invent, abbreviate, or rephrase the slug

checklist.name: Talk slug matches spec exactly
checklist.description: Any reference to the talk slug uses exactly '2026-04-16-devnexus-robocoders-judgment-day' as written in the Presentation Spec, not a shortened or modified version

checklist.name: Agent references generate-qr.py as the tool
checklist.description: The agent's plan references using generate-qr.py (the skill script), not direct use of the qrcode library or any ad-hoc QR generation approach

checklist.name: Agent distinguishes missing config from opt-out
checklist.description: The agent's response shows it understands the distinction: 'shortener key missing' means NOT CONFIGURED (needs a decision), not the same as 'shortener: none' (explicit opt-out)
